children.text: Hi! My name is Adam and I'm a freshman at senior high school.
children.cn: 嗨！我的名字是亚当，我是高中的一年级新生。
children.notes: freshman 指大学或高中的一年级新生。
children.statement: Adam是一名高一新生。
children.explanation: 文中Adam明确说自己是一个'freshman at senior high school'。

children.text: Going from junior high school to senior high school is a really big challenge.
children.cn: 从初中升入高中确实是一个很大的挑战。
children.notes: junior high school指初中，senior high school指高中。
children.statement: 对Adam来说，升入高中是一件轻松的事。
children.explanation: Adam说这是一项'really big challenge'（非常大的挑战）。

children.text: The first week was a little confusing.
children.cn: 第一周有点令人困惑。
children.notes: confusing 形容事物“令人困惑的”，修饰物；而confused形容人“感到困惑的”。
children.statement: 第一周的生活让Adam觉得很清晰明了。
children.explanation: 文中说第一周'a little confusing'（有点令人困惑）。

children.text: First, I had to think very carefully about which courses I wanted to take.
children.cn: 首先，我必须非常仔细地考虑我想选哪些课程。
children.notes: think about 考虑；take courses 选课/修课。
children.statement: Adam在选课时不需要思考，随便选了几门。
children.explanation: Adam表示他必须'think very carefully'（非常仔细地考虑）。

children.text: The school adviser helped me choose the suitable ones: maths, English, chemistry, world history, and Chinese.
children.cn: 学校顾问帮我选了适合我的课程：数学、英语、化学、世界历史和中文。
children.notes: adviser指顾问；suitable 适合的。
children.statement: 顾问帮助Adam挑选了合适的课程。
children.explanation: 文中明确写到顾问'helped me choose the suitable ones'。

children.text: I know that Chinese is a very difficult language, but I hope to be fluent when I graduate.
children.cn: 我知道中文是一门很困难的语言，但我希望毕业时能说得很流利。
children.notes: fluent 流利的；graduate 毕业。
children.statement: Adam认为中文很简单，不需要努力学。
children.explanation: Adam明知中文是'a very difficult language'（一门非常困难的语言）。

children.text: My adviser recommended that I should sign up for advanced literature because I like English and I'm good at it.
children.cn: 我的顾问建议我报名参加高级文学课，因为我喜欢英语并且擅长它。
children.notes: recommend建议/推荐，后面常接should + 动词原形的虚拟语气；sign up for报名参加。
children.statement: 顾问推荐Adam报高级文学课是因为他不擅长英语。
children.explanation: 是因为'I like English and I'm good at it'（我喜欢英语且擅长）。

children.text: I had to choose extra-curricular activities, too.
children.cn: 我也必须选择课外活动。
children.notes: extra-curricular 课外的。
children.statement: 除了选课之外，Adam还需要选择课外活动。
children.explanation: 文中'I had to choose extra-curricular activities, too'证明了这一点。

children.text: I tried to join the school football team, but the coach told me that I didn't play well enough.
children.cn: 我曾试图加入学校足球队，但教练告诉我我踢得不够好。
children.notes: well enough中enough放在副词well后面修饰。
children.statement: 教练认为Adam的足球技术已经足够入选球队了。
children.explanation: 教练说'I didn't play well enough'（踢得不够好）。

children.text: Obviously, I was unhappy, but I won't quit.
children.cn: 显然，我很不开心，但我不会放弃。
children.notes: obviously 显然地；quit 放弃/退出。
children.statement: 没能加入球队让Adam很伤心，但他决定不放弃。
children.explanation: 他说自己'unhappy'但是'won't quit'。

children.text: I'll find a way to improve on my own so that I can make the team next year.
children.cn: 我会自己找到提高的方法，以便明年能入选球队。
children.notes: improve 提高；make the team 入选球队；so that 引导目的状语从句。
children.statement: Adam指望教练主动来找他入队，不用自己练习。
children.explanation: Adam说'find a way to improve on my own'（靠自己提高）。
children.keywords: improve on my own, make the team

children.text: I joined a volunteer club instead.
children.cn: 相反，我加入了一个志愿者俱乐部。
children.notes: instead 代替，相反，通常放在句尾或用逗号隔开放在句首。
children.statement: Adam最终加入了一个志愿者俱乐部。
children.explanation: 文句说'I joined a volunteer club instead'。

children.text: Every Wednesday, we work at a soup kitchen and hand out food to homeless people in the community.
children.cn: 每周三，我们在施粥所工作，向社区里的无家可归者发放食物。
children.notes: soup kitchen 施粥所（救济站）；hand out 分发。
children.statement: 志愿者俱乐部只在周末进行活动。
children.explanation: 明确指出是'Every Wednesday'（每周三）。

children.text: I know I'll have to study harder as a senior high school student and get used to being responsible for a lot more.
children.cn: 我知道作为一名高中生我必须更加努力地学习，并且习惯承担更多的责任。
children.notes: get used to doing 习惯于做某事；be responsible for 对...负责。
children.statement: 高中生比初中生需要承担更多的责任。
children.explanation: Adam说'get used to being responsible for a lot more'（习惯承担多得多的责任）。

children.text: I'm a bit worried about keeping up with the other students in my advanced course, and it'll be quite difficult to get used to all the homework.
children.cn: 我有点担心跟不上我高级课程中的其他学生，而且要习惯所有的家庭作业会相当困难。
children.notes: keep up with 跟上；worried about 担心。
children.statement: Adam一点也不担心自己的学业和作业。
children.explanation: 他说自己'a bit worried'（有点担心）。

children.text: Still, I'm happy to be here.
children.cn: 不过，我很高兴能来到这里。
children.notes: still 在这里表示转折，“然而/尽管如此”。
children.statement: 虽然面临很多挑战，Adam依然很高兴能上高中。
children.explanation: 他用'Still, I'm happy to be here'表达了积极的态度。

children.text: Studying hard isn't always fun, but I'll be well prepared for university or whatever else comes in the future.
children.cn: 努力学习并不总是很有趣，但我会为大学或未来面临的任何事情做好充分准备。
children.notes: be well prepared for 为...做好充分准备；whatever 无论什么。
children.statement: Adam认为努力学习能为他以后的大学及未来做好准备。
children.explanation: 他说'I'll be well prepared for university or whatever else...'。

children.text: If you're reading this, you were probably born in the 2000s.
children.cn: 如果你正在阅读这篇文章，你可能出生在21世纪初（00后）。
children.notes: be born in 出生于。
children.statement: 作者认为读这篇文章的人大多是00后。
children.explanation: 因为文中说'you were probably born in the 2000s'（你可能出生在00年代）。

children.text: The oh-ohs.
children.cn: 即00年代。
children.notes: oh-ohs 指2000年至2009年这十年（00年代）。
children.statement: “the oh-ohs”指的是1990年代出生的人。
children.explanation: “the oh-ohs”指的是2000年代（2000-2009年）。

children.text: The 21st century.
children.cn: 21世纪。
children.notes: century 世纪，一百年。
children.statement: 2000年代属于21世纪。
children.explanation: 21世纪始于2001年，2000s代表21世纪初。

children.text: That would make you young, creative, connected, global, and no doubt smart.
children.cn: 这会使你年轻、有创造力、有紧密联系、全球化，并且毫无疑问非常聪明。
children.notes: connected 有联系的/互联的；no doubt 毫无疑问地。
children.statement: 作者认为00后的特征包括年轻、有创造力和聪明等。
children.explanation: 作者用'young, creative, connected, global, and no doubt smart'来形容00后。

children.text: Maybe good-looking, too.
children.cn: 可能长得也很看。
children.notes: good-looking 好看的，美貌的。
children.statement: “good-looking”在这里的意思是“善良的”。
children.explanation: “good-looking”意思是“好看的，美貌的”，而不是善良的。

children.text: Right?
children.cn: 对吧？
children.notes: Right? 用于征求对方同意，意为“对吧？”。
children.statement: 作者在这里使用“Right?”是为了表示强烈的反对语气。
children.explanation: 这是一种轻松幽默的互动问句，意为“对吧？”。

children.text: But what do other people think about your generation?
children.cn: 但是其他人对你们这一代人有什么看法呢？
children.notes: think about 考虑，对...有某种看法；generation 一代人。
children.statement: 这句话引出了下文，即其他人对00后的不同看法。
children.explanation: 这是一个过渡句，引发读者思考并引出后文其他人的看法。

children.text: Some adults worry that you're more interested in the screen in front of you than the world around you.
children.cn: 一些成年人担心你对你面前的屏幕比对你周围的世界更感兴趣。
children.notes: be interested in 对...感兴趣；in front of 在...前面。
children.statement: 成年人觉得年轻人对周围世界的关注超过了手机屏幕。
children.explanation: 相反，成年人担心年轻人'more interested in the screen... than the world around you'。

children.text: They think of you as the 'face-down generation' because you use your phone so much and they wonder how you will deal with school, friends, and family.
children.cn: 他们把你当成“低头族”，因为你太频繁地使用手机，他们想知道你将如何处理学校、朋友和家人的关系。
children.notes: think of... as... 把...看作；deal with 处理/应付。
children.statement: “低头族”是因为年轻人频繁看手机低头而得名。
children.explanation: 文中写到'think of you as the face-down generation because you use your phone so much'。

children.text: Are today's teenagers too busy texting and taking selfies to become successful in real life or 'IRL', as you would say?
children.cn: 如今的青少年是否太忙于发短信 and 自拍，而无法在现实生活（即你们所说的“IRL”）中取得成功？
children.notes: too... to... 太...以至于不能；selfie 自拍；IRL指in real life（在现实生活中）。
children.statement: “IRL”在网络语言中代表“现实生活”（In Real Life）。
children.explanation: 文中说'real life or IRL, as you would say'，确实如此。

children.text: Other adults worry that today's youth are spoilt and don't want to face the challenges of adult life.
children.cn: 另外一些成年人则担心今天的年轻人被宠坏了，不想面对成年人生活的挑战。
children.notes: spoilt 被宠坏的；face 面临/面对；adult life 成年生活。
children.statement: 一些大人认为现在的年轻人非常独立，喜欢面对挑战。
children.explanation: 文中说大人担心年轻人'spoilt and don't want to face the challenges'（被宠坏，不想面对挑战）。

children.text: Many children born in the 1990s and 2000s were raised by 'helicopter parents' who were always there to guide and help them with a busy schedule filled with homework and extra-curricular activities such as dancing, drawing, or sports.
children.cn: 许多出生在20世纪90年代 and 21世纪初的孩子是由“直升机父母”抚养长大的，他们总是守候在那里，帮孩子安排满满的日程，包括家庭作业和舞蹈、绘画、体育等课外活动。
children.notes: helicopter parents “直升机父母”，指过分干涉、保护孩子的父母；be filled with 充满。
children.statement: “直升机父母”是指放任孩子不管、从不干预孩子的家长。
children.explanation: “直升机父母”是'always there to guide and help them'，包办一切日程。

children.text: With parents who do everything for them, today's youth seem to prefer to live like teenagers even when they are in their 20s or 30s.
children.cn: 由于父母替他们包办了一切，今天的年轻人似乎即使在二三十岁时，也更喜欢像青少年一样生活。
children.notes: prefer to do 更喜欢做某事；even when 甚至当...的时候。
children.statement: 过度包办的教育可能导致年轻人在成年后依然表现得像个孩子。
children.explanation: 因为父母包办一切，他们'prefer to live like teenagers even when they are in their 20s or 30s'。

children.text: Does the face-down generation need a heads-up?
children.cn: 那么低头族需要警示/提醒吗？
children.notes: heads-up 警告，提醒，提示。
children.statement: “heads-up”在这里的意思是“抬头看”。
children.explanation: 在这里，'heads-up'是一个名词，意思是“警告、提示或提醒”。

children.text: Well, probably not.
children.cn: 好吧，可能并不需要。
children.notes: probably 很可能。
children.statement: 作者认为低头族非常需要大人的警醒和干预。
children.explanation: 作者明确回答说'Well, probably not'（大概不需要）。

children.text: The fact is that many of today's teenagers are better educated and more creative than past generations.
children.cn: 事实是，与以往几代人相比，今天的许多青少年受过更好的教育，更有创造力。
children.notes: better educated 受过更好教育的；creative 有创造力的；past generations 过去几代人。
children.statement: 现在的青少年比以前的几代人受教育水平更低。
children.explanation: 相反，现在是'better educated and more creative'（受过更好的教育且更有创造力）。

children.text: They also seem to be enthusiastic and willing to become leaders.
children.cn: 他们似乎也很有热情，愿意成为领导者。
children.notes: enthusiastic 热情的；be willing to do 乐意做某事；leader 领导者。
children.statement: 现在的年轻人看起来缺乏热情，不爱当领导。
children.explanation: 文中说他们'enthusiastic and willing to become leaders'（热情且乐意成为领导者）。

children.text: More young people than ever volunteer to help their communities.
children.cn: 比以往任何时候都多的年轻人自愿帮助他们的社区。
children.notes: than ever 比以往任何时候都...；volunteer to do 自愿做某事；community 社区。
children.statement: 如今的年轻人非常冷漠，很少参与社区志愿服务。
children.explanation: 事实是'More young people than ever volunteer'（比以往任何时候都多的人参与志愿活动）。

children.text: There are also brave young people such as Malala Yousafzai, the teenager who won the 2014 Nobel Peace Prize for pushing girls' rights to go to school.
children.cn: 还有像马拉拉·优素福扎伊这样勇敢的年轻人，她因争取女童入学权而获得了2014年诺贝尔和平奖。
children.notes: Nobel Peace Prize 诺贝尔和平奖；pushing 推动，争取；who 引导限制性定语从句修饰the teenager。
children.statement: 马拉拉因为帮助无家可归的人而获得了诺贝尔和平奖。
children.explanation: 她获奖是'for pushing girls' rights to go to school'（争取女孩上学的权利）。

children.text: So if you're one of the oh-ohs, there's reason to be hopeful about the future.
children.cn: 所以，如果你是21世纪初出生的人，我们有理由对未来充满希望。
children.notes: oh-ohs 意指2000年后出生的人（00后）；hopeful about 对...抱有希望的。
children.statement: 作者认为我们应该对00后的未来充满信心和希望。
children.explanation: 文句说'there's reason to be hopeful about the future'（有理由对未来充满希望）。

children.text: Things are looking up for the face-down generation.
children.cn: “低头族”的情况正在好转。
children.notes: look up 抬头看，（情况）好转。
children.statement: “look up”在这里的意思是好转、有起色。
children.explanation: 在句中，'Things are looking up'指事情在往好的方向发展，即情况好转。

children.text: Chances are that you will do GR8 and LOL.
children.cn: 你很可能会表现得很棒，开怀大笑。
children.notes: Chances are that 很可能...；GR8 是 great 的网络缩写；LOL 是 laugh out loud 的网络缩写。
children.statement: “GR8”和“LOL”是网络短信常用的缩写词语。
children.explanation: GR8代表Great（棒极了），LOL代表Laugh Out Loud（大笑），属于网络用语常用缩写。
